page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 12 dedicated vCPUs and 12 physical cores, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and the bottom 10% for Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tasks. However, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small and large block read/write operations, though overall Passmark memory scores fall into the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. This makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high single-threaded compute speeds. Tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage and weak single-core execution.

The instance is best suited for workloads such as database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate web serving. It can also handle light language model inference for small models, though performance degrades significantly on larger models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m8.3xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression speeds are weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, whereas multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently fall within the average range. This hardware profile provides a cost-effective option for memory-intensive applications that leverage multi-threaded processing. It is well-suited for database hosting, multi-threaded decompression, and general-purpose workloads that do not require high single-core throughput or local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring twelve dedicated cores and ninety-six gigabytes of RAM for multi-threaded workloads.

KVM hypervisor on x86_64 architecture with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
